title: Would You Pay $1 for Agentic Code Review on PRs?
excerpt: LLM-driven code reviews in CI/CD have become common SASS offerings, but where does that leave open source software projects? Here's an approach to regain complete control of the harness, models, and cost.
content_html:
It might surprise people that I’m investing time in LLM tooling — “Agentic” tooling even — because I’ve always been somewhat of an AI-skeptic. But, this code-review project is not my first foray into the category; I am also working on a task runner called camas that optionally includes an MCP and skills, and indeed it was used for the development of code-review. In turn camas now benefits from code-reviews’s agentic review on every PR.

Perhaps I am more of an “AI-industry” skeptic, or wary of hype and fear-mongering, than I am skeptical of LLMs themselves. I am not convinced that “AI agents” can replace engineers, or any occupation, really. I can only speak for my experience as a software engineer — the frontier models like Opus and Fable 5 are particularly well-suited for writing software, and on their own, I don’t think they’re that good at it. But I’m not sticking my head in the sand either. My experience is that LLMs, when bounded by expert judgement and deterministic checks, provide an incredible, albeit hard to quantify, boost to the pace of development and testing. One category where I’m very happy with their utility is code review, particularly agentic code review.

Lately, I’ve started to dread what a post-AI-bubble or post-token-subsidy world would look like. Will models that provide affordable inference survive? How will Open Source Software (OSS) projects benefit from LLMs if contributors are no longer able to use subsidized flat-rate plans?

Some definitions are in order since “agentic” is a bit of a buzz word.

#Peer Code Review

Let’s relate everything back to what code review means to you. In order to review the code, you must first read the code — additions and removals — and you may choose to also interact with the code. Interacting with the code would require copying (pulling) the changes to your local development environment and then fiddling with it: running the test suite, running the library or application, modifying code as you do so, and in my line of work — something that an LLM agent cannot do — flashing firmware to a physical device and interacting with it. The process uses aspects of engineering, design, quality assurance (QA), testing, and writing.

The kind of review that is read-only — an audit of changes — is like a “Static LLM Code Review”. A review that interacts with — or even modifies — the code is like an “Agentic LLM Code Review”.

#Static LLM Code Review

This is a relatively cheap approach to automated code review because it only reads. It’s also easy to implement: just pipe your git diff into an API chat prompt and ask for a review. There are many examples of ready made GitHub Actions to do this and it’s simple enough that you can do it yourself (something that will be a theme in this post.)

Some may claim that this is agentic because the chatbot could do things like make web searches to validate claims. Nonsense: those are other aspects of a standard read-only code review.

Still others might call an LLM doing this sort of review “static analysis”, to which I disagree fervently. Static analysis is something that software engineers are already familiar with and it is a deterministic routine to assess some qualities of the software. LLMs are stochastic not deterministic so they cannot perform what we traditionally consider static analysis…

#Agentic LLM Code Review

…unless those LLMs are given access to the deterministic tools that do perform static analysis. This is the realm of Agentic Code Review and it’s something that I have been quite impressed by ever since my first use of Claude Code’s absolute token incinerator of a command: /code-review.

An agentic code review uses a harness (like Claude Code) and can run compilers, linters, launch parallel subagents in worktrees, modify the code to confirm hypotheses, research dependencies, check for CVEs, possibly even interact with the application (though not on physical hardware for us firmware engineers 🤖).

Where a static LLM code review may come back in a few minutes after consuming less than 1M tokens, an agentic review may go on for tens of minutes, or even an hour, using 5M tokens and beyond. Which is why you’ll see Anthropic advertise this service as costing $15-$25 per PR. And frankly, I could see this going out to $100 eventually, based on the hour+ /code-review sessions I’ve run locally.

In my experience, these agentic reviews reliably surface real, sometimes quite subtle, findings. And that’s what we want. But how can we enable agentic code review at an affordable cost for OSS?

#Open Source Models

tl;dr it’s DeepSeek v4

I’m not going to claim to understand API token pricing, but what I will claim is that as long as DeepSeek is offering the rate they do on input cache hits, DeepSeek v4 Pro/Flash are an incredible value for this kind of open source agentic code review and it’s what I’ve been experimenting with for the last couple of weeks.

Here is an actual cost table from an actual agentic code review on an actual PR: $0.20 for a 14m 54s run.

DeepSeek has no charge for Cache write (AFAIK, please correct me if I’m wrong) and an incredible discount on DeepSeek v4 Pro Cache read of $0.003625/MTok, leading to an affordable review for open source.

#Wait… where’s the API key? How is it safe from forks? And how was that code review produced?

Basically I wanted this thing, and in the era of practically free inference on frontier models, I will admit that the implementation and spec is written by an LLM.

Wait, no, don’t leave!

Because there is not a precedent for agentic code review on public repos, not to mention implementation of granular time and cost constraints, I decided that the first step was to produce a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) and set it loose in the wild. If I had worked on it without LLM assistance, I’d still be drafting the spec and validating GitHub Actions security boundaries instead of publishing a write up.

#code-review

#What the MVP reference implementation accomplishes

  • Mitigates risk of leaking your API token by triage, network egress lock, and structured rendering (but you do need to use a burner key with a small amount on it, to be safe).
  • CI-reactive review paths:
    • On CI success, launches the “full review”.
    • On CI failure, launches a minimal “mechanic” review meant to guide contributors to correct mistakes. Optional, can just do no review on CI failure to avoid sinking cost.
  • Structured review output:
    • Each review will meet requirements because it is generated from markdown templates, not an LLM’s whims.
    • The LLM delivers review in a JSON document that has been schema validated.
    • LLMs reading the review are directed to that JSON document, not GH API + markdown parsing.
  • Deterministic(-ish!) time and cost limits on the agentic review. This was by far the most difficult aspect of development and stands to be improved — harness improvements would have the biggest impact here.
  • Start from a GH comment like /code-review $0.25 Make sure to check that all docs were updated
    • Limit the ability to start a code review to trusted repository members.
    • Control costs, time limits, and review focus for iterative PR phases.
  • Full control:
    • change the harness
    • change the models
    • change the prompts
    • change the supporting tooling
    • change the provided context (feed it the CI test reports, for example)
  • Incremental re-reviews: the system reads previous review JSONs to jump-start the review context
  • Traceability — the entire review transcript is uploaded

All of that is accomplished with markdown templates, the reference review-reusable.yaml, and the code-review helper tool.

Calling 5K lines of TS with 10K lines of supporting tests a “helper” might mean I’m finally vibe-pilled 🤦‍♀️.

#You Can Try it Now

  1. Create a review.yaml in your GitHub repo that imports code-review’s review-reusable.yaml and sets the options.
  2. Add .github/prices.json to get cost estimates for your reviews.
  3. Add the MODEL_API_KEY secret to your repo (your deepseek platform API key).
  4. Once this is present on main, your next PR into main will trigger the review workflow.

#The spec

So that’s the reference implementation that anyone can try out today, but the core of the repository is the idea itself, characterized in this spec.md document. I hope that this proof-of-concept inspires others to work in this category. And of course contribution to code-review is welcome!

I don’t know how the market-side could work, and I don’t know how much inference is going to cost in the long run, but personally I would share my open source projects as training data in exchange for reduced token rates.

Here are some highlights of what the specification document defines and how the MVP went about realizing them.

#GitHub Actions & Public Forks

Briefly, there are real security vulnerabilities created by allowing public forks to open a pull request that triggers github actions.

There are two primary concerns in the case of enabling agentic code review.

  1. Secret exfiltration. You at least need to have an API token as a GitHub secret, so that’s a likely target. Open a PR that modifies the GitHub workflow to print the secret to the log, then use it to pay for inference.
  2. Spoofing. Using our API token to ask the LLM to do some malicious behavior, or just plain stealing your inference for themselves.

In GitHub terms, the way that code-review mitigates risk is by running the workflow from main, not from the untrusted fork. This means that in the review context, the repository source code is unmodified by the PR’s changes. So how do we we run the review of the PR if we’re on main, not the branch corresponding to the PR?

  1. Checkout main.
  2. Install our code-review helper tool (described above) and Claude Code from npm.
  3. Run the users own environment setup steps.
  4. Lock network egress to a short trusted list.
  5. Run the security triage: a Claude Code agent evaluates the diff of the PR, with a tiny subset of tools available, and determines whether the PR is safe for a full agentic review, as true or false. This is not without risk, which is why it’s important for open source repositories to use API keys with small amounts on them — $5.00 — so that API key leakage is not a catastrophe. I am inviting everyone to try to break it on my own repositories by opening PRs against them with prompt injections.
  6. Run the agentic review, branching on the result of CI. You could always just not even start this review workflow if CI fails. Or you can run a $0.05 review to help a new contributor understand why CI failed for them.
  7. Validate the JSON schema of the review.
  8. Generate the review comments from the JSON + templates and post them to the PR.
  9. Upload the review JSON and full transcripts.

I’m not pretending it’s simple. It’s a 700+ line YAML file and that’s after abstracting lots of stuff in the code-review helper app. But let’s jump to the spelled out threat model so that we’re not GitHub-centric.

#Threat Model

The most important part of the specification is the threat model and suggestions for mitigation.

#Assets

There are the assets shared with anyone who creates a PR from a fork.

  • Model API key — spends money; the primary target.
  • Write credential — can modify the repository or its conversation; the secondary target.
  • Reviewer runtime — transient, but its environment holds the model key.

#Threats

ThreatWhy it mattersControl
Untrusted content hijacks the reviewer (direct or indirect prompt injection)The reviewer reads author-controlled text by design (OWASP LLM01; NIST)The reviewer holds no write/publish credential and no open egress; its worst output is a public comment (§2.2)
Reviewer is induced to exfiltrate the model key or environment secretsThe “lethal trifecta” turns injection into exfiltration (Willison)Read-only credential; egress allowlist (StepSecurity); burner key with hard spend cap; public comment is the sole accepted residual channel
Untrusted change runs in a privileged context (pwn request / poisoned pipeline execution)A documented, high-impact CI class (GitHub Security Lab; OWASP; Gil & Krivelevich)The privileged (write) role never executes the change; the trigger and reviewed commit come from CI, not author content
Output carries a payload aimed at the maintainer (phishing, “ignore previous instructions”)A finding is untrusted text shown to a humanRendered as data by a host that neutralizes active content; the commenter escapes all untrusted text (defense in depth)
Output smuggles a second, conflicting result to flip the verdictAppending an extra block could mask the real oneRecovery accepts exactly one validating candidate; ambiguity fails closed (§3.2)
A fork redirects the review to the wrong targetAuthor-controlled routing would misattribute the reviewTarget resolved from the trusted commit identifier (+ branch), never from author-controlled data
A prior review is spoofed to suppress a real oneA copied marker could impersonate the botPrior state trusted by authenticated author identity, not by any marker (§3.3)
Spend runaway / denial-of-walletRepeated pushes could burn budgetBurner key + hard spend cap; run timeout; cancel superseded runs
Tampered deliverable in transit between rolesThe write role must not act on forged dataTransport integrity is provided by the CI platform; the write role only deserializes, never executes

#Conclusion

I will continue to use code-review on my own OSS projects since I’ve already seen it catch issues that slipped by local review and my own (“static”) review. The maintenance workflow for it tends to be pretty simple to manage:

  • Notice a problem with a review? Point an agent to the review and ask it to create an issue, including links to the comment and run, on the code-review repo.
  • Assign one or more issues to an agent that will create a PR on code-review.
  • Instruct that agent to wait on code-review’s automated CI + code reviews, and iterate.

Looking forward to seeing more users and bug reports, or new systems based on the threat mitigations demonstrated. Good luck!

blocks.html_en: #Peer Code Review

sentences.en: #Peer Code Review
sentences.zh: #同行代码审查

blocks.html_en: Let’s relate everything back to what code review means to you. In order to review the code, you must first read the code — additions and removals — and you may choose to also interact with the code. Interacting with the code would require copying (pulling) the changes to your local development environment and then fiddling with it: running the test suite, running the library or application, modifying code as you do so, and in my line of work — something that an LLM agent cannot do — flashing firmware to a physical device and interacting with it. The process uses aspects of engineering, design, quality assurance (QA), testing, and writing.

sentences.en: Let’s relate everything back to what code review means to you.
sentences.zh: 让我们将所有内容与代码审查对您的意义联系起来。

sentences.en: In order to review the code, you must first read the code — additions and removals — and you may choose to also interact with the code.
sentences.zh: 为了审查代码，您必须首先阅读代码（添加和删除），并且您也可以选择与代码进行交互。

sentences.en: Interacting with the code would require copying (pulling) the changes to your local development environment and then fiddling with it: running the test suite, running the library or application, modifying code as you do so, and in my line of work — something that an LLM agent cannot do — flashing firmware to a physical device and interacting with it.
sentences.zh: 与代码交互需要将更改复制（拉取）到本地开发环境，然后进行修改：运行测试套件、运行库或应用程序、修改代码，而在我的工作中（LLM 代理无法做到这一点）将固件刷新到物理设备并与其交互。

sentences.en: The process uses aspects of engineering, design, quality assurance (QA), testing, and writing.
sentences.zh: 该过程涉及工程、设计、质量保证 (QA)、测试和编写等方面。

blocks.html_en: The kind of review that is read-only — an audit of changes — is like a “Static LLM Code Review”. A review that interacts with — or even modifies — the code is like an “Agentic LLM Code Review”.

sentences.en: The kind of review that is read-only — an audit of changes — is like a “Static LLM Code Review”.
sentences.zh: 只读的审查（变更审核）就像“静态 LLM 代码审查”。

sentences.en: A review that interacts with — or even modifies — the code is like an “Agentic LLM Code Review”.
sentences.zh: 与代码交互甚至修改代码的审查就像“代理 LLM 代码审查”。

blocks.html_en: #Static LLM Code Review

sentences.en: #Static LLM Code Review
sentences.zh: #Static LLM 代码审查

blocks.html_en: This is a relatively cheap approach to automated code review because it only reads. It’s also easy to implement: just pipe your git diff into an API chat prompt and ask for a review. There are many examples of ready made GitHub Actions to do this and it’s simple enough that you can do it yourself (something that will be a theme in this post.)

sentences.en: This is a relatively cheap approach to automated code review because it only reads.
sentences.zh: 这是一种相对便宜的自动代码审查方法，因为它只读取。

sentences.en: It’s also easy to implement: just pipe your git diff into an API chat prompt and ask for a review.
sentences.zh: 它也很容易实现：只需将 git diff 通过管道传输到 API 聊天提示中并请求审核即可。

sentences.en: There are many examples of ready made GitHub Actions to do this and it’s simple enough that you can do it yourself (something that will be a theme in this post.)
sentences.zh: 有许多现成的 GitHub Actions 示例可以完成此操作，而且非常简单，您可以自己完成（这将是本文的主题。）

blocks.html_en: Some may claim that this is agentic because the chatbot could do things like make web searches to validate claims. Nonsense: those are other aspects of a standard read-only code review.

sentences.en: Some may claim that this is agentic because the chatbot could do things like make web searches to validate claims.
sentences.zh: 有些人可能会声称这是代理性的，因为聊天机器人可以做一些事情，比如进行网络搜索来验证声明。

sentences.en: Nonsense: those are other aspects of a standard read-only code review.
sentences.zh: 废话：这些是标准只读代码审查的其他方面。

blocks.html_en: Still others might call an LLM doing this sort of review “static analysis”, to which I disagree fervently. Static analysis is something that software engineers are already familiar with and it is a deterministic routine to assess some qualities of the software. LLMs are stochastic not deterministic so they cannot perform what we traditionally consider static analysis…

sentences.en: Still others might call an LLM doing this sort of review “static analysis”, to which I disagree fervently.
sentences.zh: 还有一些人可能将进行此类审查的法学硕士称为“静态分析”，我对此表示强烈反对。

sentences.en: Static analysis is something that software engineers are already familiar with and it is a deterministic routine to assess some qualities of the software.
sentences.zh: 静态分析是软件工程师已经熟悉的东西，它是评估软件某些质量的确定性例程。

sentences.en: LLMs are stochastic not deterministic so they cannot perform what we traditionally consider static analysis…
sentences.zh: 法学硕士是随机的而不是确定性的，因此它们无法执行我们传统上认为的静态分析......

blocks.html_en: #Agentic LLM Code Review

sentences.en: #Agentic LLM Code Review
sentences.zh: #Agentic LLM 代码审查

blocks.html_en: …unless those LLMs are given access to the deterministic tools that do perform static analysis. This is the realm of Agentic Code Review and it’s something that I have been quite impressed by ever since my first use of Claude Code’s absolute token incinerator of a command: /code-review.

sentences.en: …unless those LLMs are given access to the deterministic tools that do perform static analysis.
sentences.zh: …除非那些法学硕士有权使用执行静态分析的确定性工具。

sentences.en: This is the realm of Agentic Code Review and it’s something that I have been quite impressed by ever since my first use of Claude Code’s absolute token incinerator of a command: /code-review.
sentences.zh: 这就是 Agentic Code Review 的领域，自从我第一次使用 Claude Code 的命令的绝对令牌焚化器：/code-review 以来，这给我留下了深刻的印象。

blocks.html_en: An agentic code review uses a harness (like Claude Code) and can run compilers, linters, launch parallel subagents in worktrees, modify the code to confirm hypotheses, research dependencies, check for CVEs, possibly even interact with the application (though not on physical hardware for us firmware engineers 🤖).

sentences.en: An agentic code review uses a harness (like Claude Code) and can run compilers, linters, launch parallel subagents in worktrees, modify the code to confirm hypotheses, research dependencies, check for CVEs, possibly even interact with the application (though not on physical hardware for us firmware engineers 🤖).
sentences.zh: 代理代码审查使用工具（如 Claude Code），可以运行编译器、linter、在工作树中启动并行子代理、修改代码以确认假设、研究依赖关系、检查 CVE，甚至可能与应用程序交互（尽管对于我们固件工程师来说不是在物理硬件上🤖）。

blocks.html_en: Where a static LLM code review may come back in a few minutes after consuming less than 1M tokens, an agentic review may go on for tens of minutes, or even an hour, using 5M tokens and beyond. Which is why you’ll see Anthropic advertise this service as costing $15-$25 per PR. And frankly, I could see this going out to $100 eventually, based on the hour+ /code-review sessions I’ve run locally.

sentences.en: Where a static LLM code review may come back in a few minutes after consuming less than 1M tokens, an agentic review may go on for tens of minutes, or even an hour, using 5M tokens and beyond.
sentences.zh: 静态 LLM 代码审查可能会在消耗少于 1M 代币后几分钟内返回，而代理审查可能会持续数十分钟，甚至一个小时，使用 500 万代币甚至更多。

sentences.en: Which is why you’ll see Anthropic advertise this service as costing $15-$25 per PR.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么您会看到 Anthropic 宣传这项服务的每个 PR 的费用为 15-25 美元。

sentences.en: And frankly, I could see this going out to $100 eventually, based on the hour+ /code-review sessions I’ve run locally.
sentences.zh: 坦率地说，根据我在本地运行的超过一小时/代码审查会议，我认为最终会达到 100 美元。

blocks.html_en: In my experience, these agentic reviews reliably surface real, sometimes quite subtle, findings. And that’s what we want. But how can we enable agentic code review at an affordable cost for OSS?

sentences.en: In my experience, these agentic reviews reliably surface real, sometimes quite subtle, findings.
sentences.zh: 根据我的经验，这些代理评论可靠地揭示了真实的、有时相当微妙的发现。

sentences.en: And that’s what we want.
sentences.zh: 这就是我们想要的。

sentences.en: But how can we enable agentic code review at an affordable cost for OSS?
sentences.zh: 但我们如何才能以可承受的成本为 OSS 启用代理代码审查呢？

blocks.html_en: I’m not going to claim to understand API token pricing, but what I will claim is that as long as DeepSeek is offering the rate they do on input cache hits, DeepSeek v4 Pro/Flash are an incredible value for this kind of open source agentic code review and it’s what I’ve been experimenting with for the last couple of weeks.

blocks.html_en: Because there is not a precedent for agentic code review on public repos, not to mention implementation of granular time and cost constraints, I decided that the first step was to produce a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) and set it loose in the wild. If I had worked on it without LLM assistance, I’d still be drafting the spec and validating GitHub Actions security boundaries instead of publishing a write up.

sentences.en: #The spec
sentences.zh: #规格

blocks.html_en: So that’s the reference implementation that anyone can try out today, but the core of the repository is the idea itself, characterized in this spec.md document. I hope that this proof-of-concept inspires others to work in this category. And of course contribution to code-review is welcome!

sentences.en: So that’s the reference implementation that anyone can try out today, but the core of the repository is the idea itself, characterized in this spec.md document.
sentences.zh: 这就是今天任何人都可以尝试的参考实现，但存储库的核心是想法本身，在这个 spec.md 文档中进行了描述。

sentences.en: I hope that this proof-of-concept inspires others to work in this category.
sentences.zh: 我希望这个概念验证能够激励其他人从事这一领域的工作。

sentences.en: And of course contribution to code-review is welcome!
sentences.zh: 当然，欢迎对代码审查做出贡献！

blocks.html_en: I don’t know how the market-side could work, and I don’t know how much inference is going to cost in the long run, but personally I would share my open source projects as training data in exchange for reduced token rates.

sentences.en: I don’t know how the market-side could work, and I don’t know how much inference is going to cost in the long run, but personally I would share my open source projects as training data in exchange for reduced token rates.
sentences.zh: 我不知道市场端如何运作，也不知道从长远来看推理会花费多少成本，但就我个人而言，我会分享我的开源项目作为训练数据，以换取降低的代币费率。

blocks.html_en: Here are some highlights of what the specification document defines and how the MVP went about realizing them.

sentences.en: Here are some highlights of what the specification document defines and how the MVP went about realizing them.
sentences.zh: 以下是规范文档定义的一些要点以及 MVP 如何实现它们。

blocks.html_en: #GitHub Actions & Public Forks

sentences.en: #GitHub Actions & Public Forks
sentences.zh: #GitHub 操作和公共分叉

blocks.html_en: Briefly, there are real security vulnerabilities created by allowing public forks to open a pull request that triggers github actions.

sentences.en: Briefly, there are real security vulnerabilities created by allowing public forks to open a pull request that triggers github actions.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，允许公共分叉打开触发 github 操作的拉取请求会产生真正的安全漏洞。

blocks.html_en: There are two primary concerns in the case of enabling agentic code review.

sentences.en: There are two primary concerns in the case of enabling agentic code review.
sentences.zh: 启用代理代码审查有两个主要问题。

blocks.html_en: • Secret exfiltration. You at least need to have an API token as a GitHub secret, so that’s a likely target. Open a PR that modifies the GitHub workflow to print the secret to the log, then use it to pay for inference.

sentences.en: Secret exfiltration.
sentences.zh: 秘密渗透。

sentences.en: You at least need to have an API token as a GitHub secret, so that’s a likely target.
sentences.zh: 您至少需要有一个 API 令牌作为 GitHub 机密，因此这可能是目标。

sentences.en: Open a PR that modifies the GitHub workflow to print the secret to the log, then use it to pay for inference.
sentences.zh: 打开一个修改 GitHub 工作流程的 PR，将秘密打印到日志中，然后用它来支付推理费用。

blocks.html_en: • Spoofing. Using our API token to ask the LLM to do some malicious behavior, or just plain stealing your inference for themselves.

sentences.en: Spoofing.
sentences.zh: 欺骗。

sentences.en: Using our API token to ask the LLM to do some malicious behavior, or just plain stealing your inference for themselves.
sentences.zh: 使用我们的 API 令牌要求法学硕士做一些恶意行为，或者只是简单地窃取您的推论。

blocks.html_en: In GitHub terms, the way that code-review mitigates risk is by running the workflow from main, not from the untrusted fork. This means that in the review context, the repository source code is unmodified by the PR’s changes. So how do we we run the review of the PR if we’re on main, not the branch corresponding to the PR?

sentences.en: In GitHub terms, the way that code-review mitigates risk is by running the workflow from main, not from the untrusted fork.
sentences.zh: 用 GitHub 的术语来说，代码审查降低风险的方式是从 main 运行工作流程，而不是从不受信任的 fork 运行工作流程。

sentences.en: This means that in the review context, the repository source code is unmodified by the PR’s changes.
sentences.zh: 这意味着在审查上下文中，存储库源代码不会因 PR 的更改而被修改。

sentences.en: So how do we we run the review of the PR if we’re on main, not the branch corresponding to the PR?
sentences.zh: 那么，如果我们在主干上，而不是在 PR 对应的分支上，我们该如何对 PR 进行审查呢？

blocks.html_en: • Checkout main.

sentences.en: Checkout main.
sentences.zh: 结账为主。

blocks.html_en: • Install our code-review helper tool (described above) and Claude Code from npm.

sentences.en: Install our code-review helper tool (described above) and Claude Code from npm.
sentences.zh: 从 npm 安装我们的代码审查帮助工具（如上所述）和 Claude Code。

blocks.html_en: • Run the users own environment setup steps.

sentences.en: Run the users own environment setup steps.
sentences.zh: 运行用户自己的环境设置步骤。

blocks.html_en: • Lock network egress to a short trusted list.

sentences.en: Lock network egress to a short trusted list.
sentences.zh: 将网络出口锁定到一个简短的受信任列表。

blocks.html_en: • Run the security triage: a Claude Code agent evaluates the diff of the PR, with a tiny subset of tools available, and determines whether the PR is safe for a full agentic review, as true or false. This is not without risk, which is why it’s important for open source repositories to use API keys with small amounts on them — $5.00 — so that API key leakage is not a catastrophe. I am inviting everyone to try to break it on my own repositories by opening PRs against them with prompt injections.

sentences.en: Run the security triage: a Claude Code agent evaluates the diff of the PR, with a tiny subset of tools available, and determines whether the PR is safe for a full agentic review, as true or false.
sentences.zh: 运行安全分类：Claude Code 代理使用一小部分可用工具评估 PR 的差异，并确定 PR 是否可以安全进行全面代理审查（真或假）。

sentences.en: This is not without risk, which is why it’s important for open source repositories to use API keys with small amounts on them — $5.00 — so that API key leakage is not a catastrophe.
sentences.zh: 这并非没有风险，这就是为什么开源存储库使用少量（5.00 美元）的 API 密钥很重要，这样 API 密钥泄漏就不会造成灾难。

sentences.en: I am inviting everyone to try to break it on my own repositories by opening PRs against them with prompt injections.
sentences.zh: 我邀请每个人尝试在我自己的存储库上通过快速注入打开针对他们的 PR 来破解它。

blocks.html_en: • Run the agentic review, branching on the result of CI. You could always just not even start this review workflow if CI fails. Or you can run a $0.05 review to help a new contributor understand why CI failed for them.

sentences.en: Run the agentic review, branching on the result of CI.
sentences.zh: 运行代理审查，根据 CI 的结果进行分支。

sentences.en: You could always just not even start this review workflow if CI fails.
sentences.zh: 如果 CI 失败，您甚至可能永远不会启动此审核工作流程。

sentences.en: Or you can run a $0.05 review to help a new contributor understand why CI failed for them.
sentences.zh: 或者，您可以运行 0.05 美元的评论来帮助新贡献者了解 CI 失败的原因。

blocks.html_en: • Validate the JSON schema of the review.

sentences.en: Validate the JSON schema of the review.
sentences.zh: 验证评论的 JSON 架构。

blocks.html_en: • Generate the review comments from the JSON + templates and post them to the PR.

sentences.en: Generate the review comments from the JSON + templates and post them to the PR.
sentences.zh: 从 JSON + 模板生成评审意见并将其发布到 PR。

blocks.html_en: • Upload the review JSON and full transcripts.

sentences.en: Upload the review JSON and full transcripts.
sentences.zh: 上传评论 JSON 和完整成绩单。

blocks.html_en: I’m not pretending it’s simple. It’s a 700+ line YAML file and that’s after abstracting lots of stuff in the code-review helper app. But let’s jump to the spelled out threat model so that we’re not GitHub-centric.

sentences.en: I’m not pretending it’s simple.
sentences.zh: 我并不是假装这很简单。

sentences.en: It’s a 700+ line YAML file and that’s after abstracting lots of stuff in the code-review helper app.
sentences.zh: 这是一个 700 多行的 YAML 文件，是在代码审查帮助器应用程序中抽象出大量内容之后生成的。

sentences.en: But let’s jump to the spelled out threat model so that we’re not GitHub-centric.
sentences.zh: 但让我们跳到详细说明的威胁模型，这样我们就不会以 GitHub 为中心。

sentences.en: #Threat Model
sentences.zh: #威胁模型

sentences.en: #Assets

sentences.en: There are the assets shared with anyone who creates a PR from a fork.

sentences.en: Model API key — spends money; the primary target.

blocks.html_en: • Write credential — can modify the repository or its conversation; the secondary target.

sentences.en: Write credential — can modify the repository or its conversation; the secondary target.
sentences.zh: 写入凭证——可以修改存储库或其对话；次要目标。

sentences.en: Reviewer runtime — transient, but its environment holds the model key.

content_html_zh:
我在法学硕士工具上投入时间——甚至是“代理”工具——可能会让人们感到惊讶，因为我一直对人工智能持怀疑态度。 但是，这个代码审查项目并不是我第一次涉足该类别。我还在开发一个名为 camas 的任务运行程序，它可以选择包含 MCP 和技能，实际上它用于代码审查的开发。 反过来，camas 现在受益于代码审查对每个 PR 的代理审查。

也许我对“人工智能行业”持怀疑态度，或者对炒作和散布恐惧持谨慎态度，而不是对法学硕士本身持怀疑态度。 我不相信“人工智能代理”真的可以取代工程师或任何职业。 我只能谈谈我作为软件工程师的经验——像 Opus 和 Fable 5 这样的前沿模型特别适合编写软件，但我认为它们本身并没有那么擅长。 但我也不会把头埋在沙子里。 我的经验是，当受到专家判断和确定性检查的限制时，法学硕士可以令人难以置信地加快开发和测试的步伐，尽管难以量化。 我对它们的实用性非常满意的一类是代码审查，特别是代理代码审查。

最近，我开始担心后人工智能泡沫或后代币补贴的世界会是什么样子。 提供负担得起的推理的模型会生存下来吗？ 如果贡献者不再能够使用补贴的统一费率计划，开源软件 (OSS) 项目将如何从法学硕士中受益？

一些定义是合理的，因为“代理”是一个流行词。

#同行代码审查

让我们将所有内容与代码审查对您的意义联系起来。 为了审查代码，您必须首先阅读代码（添加和删除），并且您也可以选择与代码进行交互。 与代码交互需要将更改复制（拉取）到本地开发环境，然后进行修改：运行测试套件、运行库或应用程序、修改代码，而在我的工作中（LLM 代理无法做到这一点）将固件刷新到物理设备并与其交互。 该过程涉及工程、设计、质量保证 (QA)、测试和编写等方面。

只读的审查（变更审核）就像“静态 LLM 代码审查”。 与代码交互甚至修改代码的审查就像“代理 LLM 代码审查”。

#Static LLM 代码审查

这是一种相对便宜的自动代码审查方法，因为它只读取。 它也很容易实现：只需将 git diff 通过管道传输到 API 聊天提示中并请求审核即可。 有许多现成的 GitHub Actions 示例可以完成此操作，而且非常简单，您可以自己完成（这将是本文的主题。）

有些人可能会声称这是代理性的，因为聊天机器人可以做一些事情，比如进行网络搜索来验证声明。 废话：这些是标准只读代码审查的其他方面。

还有一些人可能将进行此类审查的法学硕士称为“静态分析”，我对此表示强烈反对。 静态分析是软件工程师已经熟悉的东西，它是评估软件某些质量的确定性例程。 法学硕士是随机的而不是确定性的，因此它们无法执行我们传统上认为的静态分析......

#Agentic LLM 代码审查

…除非那些法学硕士有权使用执行静态分析的确定性工具。 这就是 Agentic Code Review 的领域，自从我第一次使用 Claude Code 的命令的绝对令牌焚化器：/code-review 以来，这给我留下了深刻的印象。

代理代码审查使用工具（如 Claude Code），可以运行编译器、linter、在工作树中启动并行子代理、修改代码以确认假设、研究依赖关系、检查 CVE，甚至可能与应用程序交互（尽管对于我们固件工程师来说不是在物理硬件上🤖）。

静态 LLM 代码审查可能会在消耗少于 1M 代币后几分钟内返回，而代理审查可能会持续数十分钟，甚至一个小时，使用 500 万代币甚至更多。 这就是为什么您会看到 Anthropic 宣传这项服务的每个 PR 的费用为 15-25 美元。 坦率地说，根据我在本地运行的超过一小时/代码审查会议，我认为最终会达到 100 美元。

根据我的经验，这些代理评论可靠地揭示了真实的、有时相当微妙的发现。 这就是我们想要的。 但我们如何才能以可承受的成本为 OSS 启用代理代码审查呢？

#开源模型

tl;dr 这是 DeepSeek v4

我不会声称了解 API 令牌定价，但我要声明的是，只要 DeepSeek 提供输入缓存命中率，DeepSeek v4 Pro/Flash 对于这种开源代理代码审查来说就具有令人难以置信的价值，这也是我过去几周一直在尝试的。

以下是实际 PR 上的实际代理代码审查的实际成本表：1400 万次 54 秒运行为 0.20 美元。

DeepSeek 对缓存写入不收取任何费用（据我所知，如果我错了，请纠正我），并且 DeepSeek v4 Pro 缓存读取可享受 0.003625 美元/MTok 的令人难以置信的折扣，从而获得了对开源的实惠审查。

＃等待… API 密钥在哪里？ 如何避免分叉？ 代码审查是如何产生的？

基本上我想要这个东西，在前沿模型几乎自由推理的时代，我承认实现和规范是由法学硕士编写的。

等等，不，别走！

由于没有对公共存储库进行代理代码审查的先例，更不用说实施细粒度的时间和成本限制，因此我决定第一步是生成最小可行产品（MVP）并将其放开。 如果我在没有 LLM 帮助的情况下进行研究，我仍然会起草规范并验证 GitHub Actions 安全边界，而不是发布文章。

#代码审查

#MVP 参考实现实现了什么

  • 通过分类、网络出口锁定和结构化渲染降低 API 令牌泄露的风险（但为了安全起见，您确实需要使用带有少量金额的刻录密钥）。
  • CI 反应式审查路径：在 CI 成功时，启动“全面审查”。 在 CI 失败时，启动最低限度的“机械”审查，旨在指导贡献者纠正错误。 可选的，可以不对 CI 失败进行审查，以避免沉没成本。
  • 结构化评论输出：每条评论都会满足要求，因为它是从 Markdown 模板生成的，而不是法学硕士的突发奇想。 LLM 在经过架构验证的 JSON 文档中提供审阅。 阅读评论的法学硕士将直接阅读该 JSON 文档，而不是 GH API + Markdown 解析。
  • 确定性（-ish！） 代理审查的时间和成本限制。 这是迄今为止开发中最困难的方面，有待改进——线束改进将在这里产生最大的影响。
  • 从 GH 评论开始，例如 /code-review $0.25 确保检查所有文档是否已更新 限制对受信任的存储库成员启动代码审查的能力。 控制迭代 PR 阶段的成本、时间限制和审查重点。
  • 完全控制：更改工具、更改模型、更改提示、更改支持工具、更改提供的上下文（例如，向其提供 CI 测试报告）
  • 增量重新审阅：系统读取之前的审阅 JSON 以启动审阅上下文
  • 可追溯性——上传整个审核记录

所有这些都是通过 Markdown 模板、参考 review-reusable.yaml 和代码审查帮助工具完成的。

将 5K 行 TS 和 10K 行支持测试称为“助手”可能意味着我终于充满了活力 🤦‍♀️。

#你现在就可以尝试一下

  1. 在 GitHub 存储库中创建一个 review.yaml，导入 code-review 的 review-reusable.yaml 并设置选项。
  2. 添加 .github/prices.json 以获取评论的成本估算。
  3. 将 MODEL_API_KEY 密钥添加到您的存储库（您的 deepseek 平台 API 密钥）。
  4. 一旦出现在 main 上，您的下一个 PR 将触发审核工作流程。

#规格

这就是今天任何人都可以尝试的参考实现，但存储库的核心是想法本身，在这个 spec.md 文档中进行了描述。 我希望这个概念验证能够激励其他人从事这一领域的工作。 当然，欢迎对代码审查做出贡献！

我不知道市场端如何运作，也不知道从长远来看推理会花费多少成本，但就我个人而言，我会分享我的开源项目作为训练数据，以换取降低的代币费率。

以下是规范文档定义的一些要点以及 MVP 如何实现它们。

#GitHub 操作和公共分叉

简而言之，允许公共分叉打开触发 github 操作的拉取请求会产生真正的安全漏洞。

启用代理代码审查有两个主要问题。

  1. 秘密渗透。 您至少需要有一个 API 令牌作为 GitHub 机密，因此这可能是目标。 打开一个修改 GitHub 工作流程的 PR，将秘密打印到日志中，然后用它来支付推理费用。
  2. 欺骗。 使用我们的 API 令牌要求法学硕士做一些恶意行为，或者只是简单地窃取您的推论。

用 GitHub 的术语来说，代码审查降低风险的方式是从 main 运行工作流程，而不是从不受信任的 fork 运行工作流程。 这意味着在审查上下文中，存储库源代码不会因 PR 的更改而被修改。 那么，如果我们在主干上，而不是在 PR 对应的分支上，我们该如何对 PR 进行审查呢？

  1. 结账为主。
  2. 从 npm 安装我们的代码审查帮助工具（如上所述）和 Claude Code。
  3. 运行用户自己的环境设置步骤。
  4. 将网络出口锁定到一个简短的受信任列表。
  5. 运行安全分类：Claude Code 代理使用一小部分可用工具评估 PR 的差异，并确定 PR 是否可以安全进行全面代理审查（真或假）。 这并非没有风险，这就是为什么开源存储库使用少量（5.00 美元）的 API 密钥很重要，这样 API 密钥泄漏就不会造成灾难。 我邀请每个人尝试在我自己的存储库上通过快速注入打开针对他们的 PR 来破解它。
  6. 运行代理审查，根据 CI 的结果进行分支。 如果 CI 失败，您甚至可能永远不会启动此审核工作流程。 或者，您可以运行 0.05 美元的评论来帮助新贡献者了解 CI 失败的原因。
  7. 验证评论的 JSON 架构。
  8. 从 JSON + 模板生成评审意见并将其发布到 PR。
  9. 上传评论 JSON 和完整成绩单。

我并不是假装这很简单。 这是一个 700 多行的 YAML 文件，是在代码审查帮助器应用程序中抽象出大量内容之后生成的。 但让我们跳到详细说明的威胁模型，这样我们就不会以 GitHub 为中心。

#威胁模型

该规范最重要的部分是威胁模型和缓解建议。

＃资产

任何通过分叉创建 PR 的人都可以共享资产。

  • 模型API密钥——花钱；主要目标。
  • 写入凭证——可以修改存储库或其对话；次要目标。
  • Reviewer 运行时 — 短暂的，但其环境持有模型密钥。

#威胁

威胁为什么这很重要控制
不受信任的内容劫持审阅者（直接或间接提示注入）审稿人按设计读取作者控制的文本（OWASP LLM01；NIST）审阅者没有写入/发布凭证，也没有开放出口；其最糟糕的输出是公众评论（§2.2）
审阅者被诱导泄露模型密钥或环境秘密“致命三连胜”将注入变成渗透（Willison）只读凭证；出口允许列表（StepSecurity）；带硬支出帽的燃烧器钥匙；公众意见是唯一接受的剩余渠道
不受信任的更改在特权上下文中运行（pwn 请求/中毒的管道执行）一个有记录的、高影响力的 CI 课程（GitHub 安全实验室；OWASP；Gil & Krivelevich）特权（写入）角色从不执行更改；触发器和审查的提交来自 CI，而不是作者内容
输出携带针对维护者的有效负载（网络钓鱼，“忽略先前的指令”）发现结果是向人类显示的不受信任的文本由主机呈现为数据，中和活动内容；评论者转义所有不受信任的文本（深度防御）
输出偷带了第二个相互矛盾的结果来推翻判决附加额外的块可能会掩盖真实的块Recovery 只接受一个验证候选者；歧义无法解决（§3.2）
分叉将评论重定向到错误的目标作者控制的路由会错误地分配评论从受信任的提交标识符（+分支）解析目标，而不是从作者控制的数据解析
之前的评论被欺骗以压制真实的评论复制的标记可能会冒充机器人先前状态由经过验证的作者身份信任，而不是由任何标记信任（§3.3）
支出失控/钱包被拒绝重复推动可能会消耗预算燃烧器钥匙+硬支出上限；运行超时；取消被取代的运行
角色之间传输过程中的可交付成果被篡改写入角色不得对伪造数据进行操作传输完整性由CI平台提供； write角色只反序列化，从不执行

＃结论

我将继续在我自己的 OSS 项目上使用代码审查，因为我已经看到它发现了本地审查和我自己的（“静态”）审查漏掉的问题。 它的维护工作流程往往非常易于管理：

  • 注意到评论有问题吗？ 将代理指向审查并要求其在代码审查存储库上创建问题，包括评论和运行的链接。
  • 将一个或多个问题分配给将在代码审查时创建 PR 的代理。
  • 指示该代理等待代码审查的自动化 CI + 代码审查，然后进行迭代。

期待看到更多的用户和错误报告，或基于所展示的威胁缓解的新系统。 祝你好运！
